texto: Anna woke with the wonderful feeling bad sleepers have when they know they have slept well. As if they have stolen something and got away with it. At these times the memories of what led up to such deep sleep keep their distance for a few seconds and those few seconds are perhaps the only time the world can ever be said to show mercy.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a James Meek: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “Anna woke with the wonderful feeling bad sleepers have when they know they have slept well. As if…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que tempo, memory, mercy pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de mercy e sleep.

texto: He'd shot and beaten people because he couldn't talk to them.Violence was the only language nobody could understand. There were no translators.

texto: And you thought: they're used to it. But that was how those who suffered less always thought about those who suffered more, that they were used to it, that they no longer felt it as you did. Nobody ever got used to it. All they learned to do was to stop letting it show.
